name: New York
description:
New York City, often called the Big Apple, is the most populous city in the United States and one of the most influential cities in the world. Spread across five boroughs — Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, the Bronx, and Staten Island — the city is a global hub for finance, art, fashion, media, and entertainment.

Manhattan's skyline is among the most recognizable in the world, dominated by iconic skyscrapers including the Empire State Building, One World Trade Center, and the Chrysler Building. Central Park, an 843-acre oasis of green in the heart of Manhattan, offers walking trails, lakes, a zoo, and open-air theaters, providing a tranquil escape from the surrounding urban density.

The city's cultural institutions are world-class. The Metropolitan Museum of Art houses over two million works spanning 5,000 years of history. The Museum of Modern Art (MoMA) features masterpieces of modern and contemporary art. Broadway, centered in Times Square, is the pinnacle of American theater, with over 40 professional theaters staging musicals and plays nightly.

New York is one of the most ethnically diverse cities on Earth, and this diversity is reflected in its extraordinary food scene. You can find authentic cuisine from virtually every country in the world, from Chinatown's dim sum to Little Italy's pasta, from Harlem's soul food to Jackson Heights' South Asian restaurants. The city is also famous for its New York-style pizza, bagels, and cheesecake.

The Statue of Liberty, a gift from France in 1886, stands on Liberty Island in New York Harbor as a symbol of freedom and democracy. Brooklyn Bridge, completed in 1883, connects Manhattan to Brooklyn and offers stunning views of the skyline for those who walk across it.
best_time: April to June, September to November
fun_fact: The New York City subway system has 472 stations, more than any other subway system in the world.
